claim: Independent verification confirms the explorer claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch correctly implements WebFetch/WebSearch support with scope separation and validates project facts require project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES set contains exactly four scopes: project, external, user, unknown
evidence.why: Foundational scope definition confirms the explorer scope enumeration is accurate.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors() iterates through evidence array and validates each record has a valid scope, returning error messages for violations
evidence.why: Validation logic implementation confirms scope enforcement is not just declarative.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt explicitly requires scope declaration and instructs to mark WebFetch/WebSearch results as scope=external
evidence.why: Agent instructions match the claim about external research tool usage and scope marking.

evidence.summary: Line 275 instructs to use WebFetch/WebSearch and mark as scope=external; line 276 adds tool-profile-specific instruction for research tools
evidence.why: Conditional external research instructions are correctly implemented and scoped.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() implements multi-layer scope detection: explicit scope keyword, URL presence, source type prefixes, and path fallback
evidence.why: Automatic scope classification logic is more sophisticated than explorer summary suggested.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope() checks for valid scope field presence using VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES set
evidence.why: Validation function implementation confirms explicit scope requirement is enforced.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator() validates non-URL path field exists for project evidence records
evidence.why: Project locator requirement enforcement is implemented as claimed.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() returns blockers when project facts lack project-scoped evidence or lack path locators
evidence.why: Blocking logic implementation confirms the prevention of external evidence satisfying project requirements.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult() applies EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker when evidenceScopeErrors() returns issues
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement confirms scope validation happens during agent result normalization.

evidence.summary: Doctrine explicitly states external evidence from WebFetch/WebSearch can only serve as background research, not proof of project implementation
evidence.why: Product-level documentation confirms external evidence cannot substitute for project evidence.

evidence.summary: Test removes scope field from evidence and verifies EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker is applied
evidence.why: Test implementation confirms scope enforcement is actually tested and working.

evidence.summary: Test verifies agent_tool_profile=research sets agent_tool_profile to research and keeps Claude tools unrestricted
evidence.why: Test confirms research profile behavior matches implementation design.

risks: If external research tools are incorrectly used to fetch project-local files (e.g., file:// URLs or localhost endpoints), the automatic scope detection could misclassify project-local content as external.
Agent effectiveness depends on provider compliance with scope marking instructions; non-compliant providers could bypass external/project separation before validation catches it.

result.notes: All 10 explorer evidence points independently verified. The implementation correctly supports WebFetch/WebSearch for external research with scope=external marking, validates project facts require project-scoped evidence with concrete path locators, and prevents external evidence from satisfying project requirements through blocking validation chains.
